description_long: This jewelry store features a prominent brick facade with large, multi-pane windows that wrap around the front and sides, allowing ample natural light into the interior. The front entrance is recessed, flanked by two bay windows, and topped with a green sign above the door. The rear section of the building is a modern glass extension, likely serving as a showroom or display area, with a transparent roof and walls. Inside, the store is divided into distinct areas, including a main sales floor, a counter, and what appears to be a back office or storage space. The interior walls are decorated with patterned wallpaper and there are several display cases and counters visible. The overall design blends traditional brickwork with contemporary glass architecture, creating a visually appealing and inviting retail environment.
description: A jewelry store with a brick facade, large bay windows, and a modern glass extension at the rear, blending classic and contemporary architectural elements.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, large bay windows, and green signage above the door.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the brick wall, patterned wallpaper inside, and the full length of the glass extension at the rear.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the transparent glass extension and interior layout, including display areas and counters.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the solid brick wall, partial view of the interior, and the glass extension.
attribute_storefront_signage: A green rectangular sign with white text is mounted above the main entrance, centered between the two bay windows. The sign appears to be made of painted metal or plastic.
